IN1.observed: Both self and bd-ready paths admitted only eligible confirmed/pass/readiness-complete active tasks; waiting, draft, unconfirmed, evaluation-fail, and readiness-incomplete fixtures were absent from ready_set.

test_refs: independent: direct schedule-graph.py mixed dependency fixture
OUT1.observed: The direct fixture admitted `eligible` only after its `done` dependency and rejected `waiting` with a missing/non-done dependency; the pytest scenario confirmed the same behavior for both ready sources.

OUT2.observed: Two ready tasks sharing `resource_scope.touches=[web]` were placed into separate batches; an active lease on that scope caused the affected candidates to be reported as conflicts instead of recommendations.

OUT3.observed: Each admitted task received a unique `devgraph/<graph_node_id>` branch and public `/dev-graph worktree claim` command. Repeating the same C27 Beads claim kept one external bridge claim, while a failed claim released the reservation.
